title: Wireless Link Encryption Downgrade for Store WLAN Carrying Payment Terminals

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 2.2.4 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 2.2.4 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.26: Wireless Link Encryption Downgrade for Store WLAN Carrying Payment Terminals.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.26' running on index=wireless sourcetype="wlan:controller" — SSID, security mode, AP name, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Cisco (WLAN syslog), custom Meraki `meraki:radio` if used
spl:
index=wireless sourcetype="wlan:controller" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_store_ssid.csv ssid OUTPUT carries_pos
| where carries_pos="true"
| where match(_raw,"(?i)open|OWE|WEP|WPA[^2]|TKIP")
| stats count by ap_name, ssid, security_mode
| sort - count
description: Surfaces association frames or controller logs showing legacy WPA or open auth on SSIDs used by payment terminals, evidencing review of wireless encryption for account data environments.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes wireless and sourcetypes wlan:controller. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Classify SSIDs that can see payment devices. (2) Exclude guest SSIDs with captive portal if physically isolated. (3) Integrate with store opening checklist. (4) Weekly store ops digest. Indexes required: `index=wireless`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=wlan:controller`; Lookups: `pci_store_ssid`, `pci_store_ssid.csv`.
visualization: Table (SSID, security_mode), map of APs if geo available, bar chart (by store region).
knownFalsePositives: CDE access from authorized payment ops, scheduled batch reconciliation, or PCI assessor activities.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Cisco (WLAN syslog), custom Meraki `meraki:radio` if used.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=wireless` `sourcetype="wlan:controller"` — SSID, security mode, AP name.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Classify SSIDs that can see payment devices. (2) Exclude guest SSIDs with captive portal if physically isolated. (3) Integrate with store opening checklist. (4) Weekly store ops digest.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wireless sourcetype="wlan:controller" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_store_ssid.csv ssid OUTPUT carries_pos
| where carries_pos="true"
| where match(_raw,"(?i)open|OWE|WEP|WPA[^2]|TKIP")
| stats count by ap_name, ssid, security_mode
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Wireless Link Encryption Downgrade for Store WLAN Carrying Payment Terminals (PCI DSS Req 4.1.1, 2.2.4)** — Surfaces association frames or controller logs showing legacy WPA or open auth on SSIDs used by payment terminals, evidencing review of wireless encryption for account data environments.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=wireless` `sourcetype="wlan:controller"` — SSID, security mode, AP name. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Cisco (WLAN syslog), custom Meraki `meraki:radio` if used. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wireless; **sourcetype**: wlan:controller. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wireless, sourcetype="wlan:controller", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where carries_pos="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(_raw,"(?i)open|OWE|WEP|WPA[^2]|TKIP")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by ap_name, ssid, security_mode** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (SSID, security_mode), map of APs if geo available, bar chart (by store region).
grandmaExplanation: We surface association frames or controller logs showing legacy WPA or open auth on SSIDs used by payment terminals, evidencing review of wireless encryption for account data environments. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
